instruction: Find emails in Inbox from senders matching "security.example.com" (search across all pages), then save JSON to "$HOME/Desktop/mail_sender_8_1.json". Return JSON only (no extra text). Required keys: folder (string), sender_pattern (string), subjects (array of strings). Do not include extra keys.
reference_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then jq -n --arg f "Inbox" --arg p "security.example.com" '{folder:$f,sender_pattern:$p,subjects:[]}' > "$HOME/Desktop/mail_sender_8_1.json"; else printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s --arg f "Inbox" --arg p "security.example.com" 'add | {folder:$f,sender_pattern:$p,subjects:([ .[] | select((.from.addr // "") | test($p;"i")) | (.subject // "") ] | map(select(length>0)))}' > "$HOME/Desktop/mail_sender_8_1.json"; fi

parameters.expected_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then jq -n --arg f "Inbox" --arg p "security.example.com" '{folder:$f,sender_pattern:$p,subjects:[]}'; else printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s --arg f "Inbox" --arg p "security.example.com" 'add | {folder:$f,sender_pattern:$p,subjects:([ .[] | select((.from.addr // "") | test($p;"i")) | (.subject // "") ] | map(select(length>0)))}'; fi
